faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a yellow jacket nest in South Jamaica?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service throughout South Jamaica, Queens. Most yellow jacket nest removals are completed within 1-2 hours, depending on nest size and location. We understand the urgency of stinging insect issues and prioritize rapid response for South Jamaica residents.

faqs.question: What does the $350-$550 yellow jacket removal service include?
faqs.answer: Our service includes complete nest inspection, safe removal using professional-grade treatments, nest elimination, and a follow-up guarantee. Pricing depends on nest size, location accessibility, and height. Ground nests typically cost less, while nests in walls, attics, or high eaves may be at the higher end of the range.

faqs.question: Are yellow jackets dangerous to my South Jamaica property?
faqs.answer: Yes, yellow jackets are aggressive defenders of their nests and can sting multiple times, posing serious risks especially to children, pets, and those with allergies. Unlike honeybees, they become more aggressive in late summer and fall. Professional removal is the safest option to avoid painful stings and potential medical emergencies.
